ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Singaporean onion prata; closest defensible match is the generic flatbread food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E (Flat bread) is the closest defensible category; no Singapore-specific prata code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for onion prata or roti prata as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of onion prata as typically served at a Singapore hawker stall weighs approximately 100 g and is commonly eaten with curry dipping sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Onion Prata — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Onion Prata (cr_id G00010313). All per-100g macros and available micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported in the HPB entry; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous flatbread entry (pan-fried paratha).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous flatbread / paratha estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Singaporean onion prata exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 62 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: plain paratha / roti (wheat flour flatbread, pan-fried), listed at approximately 62 in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2021). Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (62 × 75.23) / 100 = 46.6. The high dietary fibre (10.2 g/100 g) may modestly lower the effective GI in practice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632 mg per 100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid onion prata due to its high sodium content of 632 mg/100 g; if consumed, restrict to a small portion and avoid high-sodium curry dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate (75.2 g/100 g) partially offset by high dietary fibre (10.2 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.9 g/100 g).
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes may consume onion prata in small portions (one piece) paired with a protein-rich curry, as the high fibre content (10.2 g/100 g) moderates the glycemic impact of the high total carbohydrate load; sweet condiments should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (632 mg/100 g) and moderate saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g) are the primary cardiovascular concerns; cholesterol is 0 mg/100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit onion prata intake due to its high sodium content and moderate saturated fat; choosing oil-cooked rather than ghee-cooked preparations and limiting portion size to one piece reduces cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 632 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold, promoting fluid retention and worsening joint inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit onion prata due to its high sodium content (632 mg/100 g), which can exacerbate tissue swelling; requesting reduced-salt preparation and pairing with anti-inflammatory condiments such as turmeric-based curry may partially mitigate risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Onion prata is a popular Singaporean hawker dish consisting of a flaky, layered flatbread made from wheat flour, water, salt, and oil or ghee, pan-fried on a hot griddle and filled or topped with sliced onion. It is a variant of the plain roti prata, which itself is derived from the South Asian paratha tradition brought to Singapore by Indian Muslim immigrants. The bread is characterised by its crispy, golden-brown exterior and soft, chewy interior layers, with the onion adding a mild sweetness and aromatic depth. Onion prata is typically served with a fish or mutton curry dipping sauce and is a common breakfast, lunch, or dinner item at Indian Muslim hawker stalls across Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 412 kcal, 9.85 g protein, 8.25 g fat, 75.23 g carbohydrate, 10.2 g dietary fibre, 2.92 g sugar, and 632 mg sodium. The high dietary fibre content is notable for a flatbread and partially moderates the glycemic impact of the high carbohydrate load. However, the sodium content of 632 mg/100 g is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 62 (medium), based on analogous paratha data. Onion prata is vegetarian and halal, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences in Singapore's multicultural food landscape.
common_user_queries: How many calories in onion prata?
Is onion prata high in sodium?
What is the GI of onion prata?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the canonical HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Onion Prata (cr_id G00010313) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from a USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous flatbread entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: Fields set to -1 and basis for unavailability: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g set to -1 — detailed fatty acid fractions are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Onion Prata (cr_id G00010313), and no sufficiently close analogous entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy provides a complete fatty acid breakdown for a Singapore-style onion prata as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_e_mg set to -1 — vitamin profile data for onion prata are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food, and no sufficiently close USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry provides a complete vitamin panel for Singapore-style onion prata as of 2026. phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg set to -1 — these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food, and no sufficiently close USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry provides these values for Singapore-style onion prata as of 2026. water_g set to -1 — moisture content is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food as of 2026. saturated_fat_g (2.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pan-fried paratha (closest analogous flatbread), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as reported by HPB; confirmed zero per the HPB source — the recipe uses vegetable oil rather than egg or dairy in the HPB formulation. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate based on plain paratha / roti from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Singaporean onion prata is available. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (62 × 75.23) / 100 = 46.6 for a 100 g serving.
